_about: Per-quest provenance metadata for every quest in the dashboard. Generated from research into the newserv-bundled quest catalog + community packs. Keys are decimal quest numbers as strings.

_schema.classify: 'original' (Sega-authored) or 'custom' (community-authored). The two-source binary the dashboard surfaces.
_schema.name: English quest name (or original language if not localized).
_schema.author: Sega / Sonic Team for originals; specific handle/name for community quests (e.g. 'soulja224466', 'Matt Swift', 'Aleron Ives').
_schema.year: Approximate year of original release. null if unknown.
_schema.sourceUrl: Authoritative reference URL for the quest (Ephinea wiki, PSO-World article, original forum thread, GitHub commit, etc.).
_schema.category: newserv's quest-menu category name (matches CategoryID lookup).
_schema.aliases: Optional list of alternate names this quest is known by (short forms, source-filename variants, case/spacing variants). Used by scripts/quest-dedup-check.py to catch duplicate installs across naming conventions.

_research_notes: Catalog audit: 243 of 260 quests are Sega-authored (classify=original). 17 are community-authored (classify=custom).

The 17 custom quests are: q000 (soulja224466, 2006), q075 (Aleron Ives's 'Aberrant Grove', brought in by Matt Swift 2025), q253 (Matt Swift's 'Story Flag Fixer' utility, 2024), and the 14 Solo Challenge quests Matt Swift added in 2024 (q8811-q8819 Ep1, q8821-q8825 Ep2) which take Sega's multiplayer-only Challenge mode and make it solo-playable.

Earlier counts of '2 custom' were wrong — the Solo Challenge mode quests were missed because they live in the challenge-solo-ep1/ep2 categories rather than the download/ folder, and q075's reclassification took confirming Matt Swift's git commit log.

Year distribution: 20 from 2000 (DC v1), 57 from 2001 (DC v2 + events), 80 from 2003 (GC), 99 from 2004 (BB Sega-originals), 1 from 2006 (q000 soulja224466), 15 from 2024 (Matt Swift contributions), 1 from 2025 (Aleron Ives via Matt Swift).

2026-06-18 update: installed 28 new Ephinea custom quests (q605-q668) from the phantasmal-world mirror, and reclassified 29 already-bundled Ephinea quests (Endless Nightmare, Phantasmal World, War of Limits, New Mop-Up Operation, Point of Disaster, The Robots' Reckoning, Maximum Attack Ver2/4th Stage) from original->custom. Catalog now tracks 74 custom of 288 total entries. All Ephinea quests are Blue Burst format (BB clients only).

2026-06-18 CORRECTION audit: the earlier same-day batch over-corrected. Reverted 20 Sega-original quests (Endless Nightmare q108-111, Phantasmal World q233-236, Point of Disaster q709, The Robots' Reckoning q710, War of Limits q811-815, New Mop-Up Operation q816-820) that were wrongly flipped to custom — confirmed Sega via Sylverant quest-list + Ephinea wiki (Author: Sonic Team). Flipped 5 genuinely-community MA4 quests (q147, q497-500) original->custom. Removed 3 installed duplicates (q628-630 == bundled q497-499; the alias gap that motivated the new aliases field). Refined 20 author attributions and added aliases to 32 entries. Custom now 56/285.

0.name: Tower Mop Up 3/11/06
0.author: soulja224466
0.notes: Custom GC-era community quest by soulja224466 (PSO GameCube modding scene contributor circa 2004-2006). Bundled in newserv since the initial commit (October 2018). 'Client: soulja224466' is recorded in the in-game quest description. The original distribution page at fuzziqersoftware.com is no longer reachable (expired SSL); the GitHub-hosted bundled file is the most stable source.

253.name: Story Flag Fixer
253.author: Matt Swift
253.platform: All retail (DC v1/v2, PC, GameCube, Xbox)
253.notes: Custom utility quest by Matt Swift (newserv contributor). Speaks to NPC 'Irene' and offers to repair offline story progression flags when Tyrell won't let a character advance — a handy fix for stuck saves. Added 2024-05-24 in the 'Add custom content and backports for V1 to V3' commit alongside the Solo Challenge series. Ships in download/ for every retail client version.

494.name: MAXIMUM ATTACK 2 Ver2
494.author: Ephinea
494.notes: Sega Virtual Reality lobby teleport quest. Authorship is Ephinea staff/community; some Maximum Attack variants derive from earlier Schthack community quests.

497.name: Maximum Attack 4th Stage -2A-
497.author: Community (Schthack / Ephinea, credited "Matt")

The Maximum Attack 4th Stage (MA4) series originated on Schthack and other private servers and is hosted by Ephinea (wiki credits "Matt"); Sega never produced an official MA4. Reclassified original->custom 2026-06-18 for consistency with the rest of the MA4 series (q144-146, q303-305).

709.name: Point of Disaster

8811.name: Stage1 - Solo
8811.author: Matt Swift
8811.platform: All retail (DC v2, PC, GameCube, Xbox)
8811.notes: Solo-playable backport of Sega's multiplayer Challenge mode Episode 1 Stage 1. Sega never released Challenge mode in solo form — Matt Swift built the solo variants as a custom newserv-bundled extension. Same scenario script as the multiplayer Challenge run, retuned to be completable single-player. Added 2024-05-24 in the 'custom content and backports for V1 to V3' commit.
